suggestions.title: Turn the accidental uv scaffold into a real qzcli-mcp package
suggestions.description: This checkout has fresh untracked `uv` files that currently declare Python 3.14, version 0.1.0, and a Hello World entrypoint. Converting them into a real `qzcli` package would directly support your recent `dev-public` access workflow through Codex/MCP.
suggestions.prompt: Replace the accidental `uv init` scaffold in this repo with a real `pyproject.toml` for `qzcli`: keep Python compatibility aligned with the existing package, declare the current dependencies and console scripts (`qzcli`, `qzcli-mcp`), remove the `main.py` hello-world stub if it is not needed, and make the project installable with `uv` without breaking the existing CLI.

suggestions.title: Close the last invalid_grant auth path from issue #14
suggestions.description: The repo still has one open GitHub issue, `#14`, and the unreleased changelog says the `/auth/token` `invalid_grant` path remains open even after this month's auth-retry merges. Codex can reproduce that bug, patch it, and add the regression coverage now.
suggestions.prompt: Trace every remaining code path that can still fall back to `/auth/token` for CAS-federated users, reproduce the `invalid_grant` behavior described in GitHub issue #14, and implement the smallest fix plus regression tests so `qzcli` stays on the cookie/CAS path wherever possible.
